_description: Example hook configurations for the retrospect trigger pipeline. These hooks are OPTIONAL enhancements — the core trigger mechanism also runs inline in workflow-protocol.md checkpoints.
_platform_note: Adapt the command path and Python executable to your environment. This file is a reference, not a runtime dependency.
_pipeline: Step 1: detector outputs JSON → Step 2: recorder writes it to 09-run-log.md

hooks.name: retrospect-detect-and-record-after-user-input
hooks.purpose: Detect user corrections or retrospective intent → automatically write to 09-run-log.md. Normal polish/formatting requests pass through as T0 without writing.

hooks.name: retrospect-detect-and-record-after-review
hooks.purpose: After independent review, scan P0/P1 findings for gate failures → auto-write to run-log. T3 gate failures auto-mark needs_retrospect.

hooks.name: retrospect-detect-and-record-after-sweep
hooks.purpose: After content consistency sweep, auto-record recurring inconsistency patterns to run-log.

forbidden_behaviors: hooks MUST NOT modify PRD body content
hooks MUST NOT modify any Skill reusable file
hooks MUST NOT bypass user confirmation for Skill changes
hooks MUST NOT auto-apply retrospective patches
hooks MUST NOT elevate a one-time preference to a universal rule without user confirmation
recorder writes ONLY to 09-run-log.md — never to PRD files or Skill files

runtime_requirements.dependencies: standard library only (argparse, json, re, datetime, pathlib)
runtime_requirements.prerequisite: .prd-workflow/current-task.json must be set with task_folder and run_log_path fields
